sourceRootLabel: AgenC WP-04 checklist, daemon JSON-RPC dispatch contract, and portal workspace UI

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The daemon JSON-RPC dispatcher handles portal session.list and session.attach requests with the existing session manager and registers attached browser clients with the session multiplexer when notification delivery is available.
edgeCases: session.list rejects non-numeric limits before reaching the session manager
session.attach rejects missing sessionId before creating attachments
client registration failure does not leave a newly registered multiplexer client behind
clients already tracked by the same daemon connection can attach to another session without duplicate registration

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The daemon JSON-RPC dispatcher handles message.send by validating content, forwarding it to the background runner with a stable messageId, and returning the acceptedAt timestamp without exposing the streaming-only result shape.
edgeCases: structured text and image blocks use the same validator as message.stream
clientMessageId is preserved as the daemon messageId
displayUserMessage metadata is validated under the message.send method name
message.send errors name message.send instead of message.stream when required services are unavailable

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The shared portal protocol allowlist and capability flags include session attach, agent attach, transcript read, and message send workflows plus helper request builders that serialize the daemon JSON-RPC envelopes exactly.
edgeCases: portal method guards still reject permission and tool mutation methods
initialize advertises the same workspace capability flags the sibling portal sends
dashboard snapshots include transcript and composer state so the UI can preserve workspace context across refreshes

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The sibling portal renders session and agent attach controls, transcript actions, a transcript view, and a session composer backed by daemon session.attach, agent.attach, agent.logs, and message.send.
edgeCases: hostile daemon strings continue to render through textContent instead of HTML injection
message send is disabled until a session is attached
session transcript actions are disabled when a session has no agentId
message sends include display metadata and refresh the selected agent transcript when possible

intentionalReductions: WP-04 does not add a hosted portal backend; browser actions continue to target the initialized daemon WebSocket from WP-02.
WP-04 does not add tool approval, permission editing, or agent stop controls. Those daemon mutations remain outside the portal workspace surface.
The transcript view reads agent.logs snapshots instead of subscribing to live event.message_chunk notifications; live streaming remains a later portal concern.

mergeResolutions.context: WP-04 uncovered daemon protocol methods that were advertised but not dispatched for browser workspace use.
mergeResolutions.resolution: Added dispatcher support for session.list, session.attach, and message.send, and passed the live session manager into the foreground daemon dispatcher.
